---
title: Hot Module Replacement
description: Update modules in the browser without a full page reload
---

Hot Module Replacement (HMR) exchanges, adds, or removes modules while an application is running, without a full reload. This significantly speeds up development.

## How It Works

From `HotModuleReplacementPlugin.js`, HMR provides runtime API:

```javascript HotModuleReplacementPlugin.js
class HotModuleReplacementPlugin {
  apply(compiler) {
    if (compiler.options.output.strictModuleErrorHandling === undefined) {
      compiler.options.output.strictModuleErrorHandling = true;
    }
    
    const runtimeRequirements = [RuntimeGlobals.module];
    
    compiler.hooks.compilation.tap(
      'HotModuleReplacementPlugin',
      (compilation, { normalModuleFactory }) => {
        // Inject module.hot API
        normalModuleFactory.hooks.parser
          .for('javascript/auto')
          .tap('HotModuleReplacementPlugin', (parser) => {
            parser.hooks.evaluateIdentifier
              .for('module.hot')
              .tap('HotModuleReplacementPlugin', (expr) => {
                return evaluateToIdentifier(
                  'module.hot',
                  'module',
                  () => ['hot'],
                  true
                )(expr);
              });
          });
      }
    );
  }
}
```

## Enabling HMR

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="webpack-dev-server">
    ```javascript webpack.config.js
    module.exports = {
      devServer: {
        hot: true  // Enable HMR
      }
    };
    ```
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="Plugin">
    ```javascript webpack.config.js
    const webpack = require('webpack');
    
    module.exports = {
      plugins: [
        new webpack.HotModuleReplacementPlugin()
      ]
    };
    ```
  </Tab>
  
  <Tab title="Node.js API">
    ```javascript
    const webpack = require('webpack');
    const config = require('./webpack.config.js');
    
    const compiler = webpack(config);
    
    compiler.watch({}, (err, stats) => {
      // HMR updates on file changes
    });
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## module.hot API

From `HotModuleReplacementPlugin.js`, the runtime API:

```javascript Application Code
if (module.hot) {
  // Accept updates for this module
  module.hot.accept();
  
  // Accept updates for specific dependencies
  module.hot.accept('./dependency', () => {
    // Handle the updated dependency
  });
  
  // Accept multiple dependencies
  module.hot.accept(['./dep1', './dep2'], () => {
    // Handle updates
  });
  
  // Decline updates (force full reload)
  module.hot.decline();
  
  // Decline specific dependencies
  module.hot.decline('./dependency');
  
  // Dispose handler (cleanup before update)
  module.hot.dispose((data) => {
    // Save state before module is replaced
    data.prevState = getCurrentState();
  });
  
  // Get data from dispose
  if (module.hot.data) {
    restoreState(module.hot.data.prevState);
  }
  
  // Remove handler
  module.hot.removeDisposeHandler(handler);
  
  // Check status
  const status = module.hot.status();
  // Possible values: 'idle', 'check', 'prepare', 'ready', 'dispose', 'apply', 'abort', 'fail'
  
  // Add status handler
  module.hot.addStatusHandler((status) => {
    console.log('HMR Status:', status);
  });
}
```

## Accept Handler

From the HMR plugin implementation:

```javascript HotModuleReplacementPlugin.js
const createAcceptHandler = (parser, ParamDependency) => {
  return (expr) => {
    const module = parser.state.module;
    const dep = new ConstDependency(
      `${module.moduleArgument}.hot.accept`,
      expr.callee.range,
      runtimeRequirements
    );
    dep.loc = expr.loc;
    module.addPresentationalDependency(dep);
    module.buildInfo.moduleConcatenationBailout = 'Hot Module Replacement';
    
    if (expr.arguments.length >= 1) {
      const arg = parser.evaluateExpression(expr.arguments[0]);
      let params = [];
      
      if (arg.isString()) {
        params = [arg];
      } else if (arg.isArray()) {
        params = arg.items.filter((param) => param.isString());
      }
      
      const requests = [];
      if (params.length > 0) {
        for (const [idx, param] of params.entries()) {
          const request = param.string;
          const dep = new ParamDependency(request, param.range);
          dep.optional = true;
          dep.loc = Object.create(expr.loc);
          dep.loc.index = idx;
          module.addDependency(dep);
          requests.push(request);
        }
      }
    }
    
    return true;
  };
};
```

## HMR Runtime

From `HotModuleReplacementRuntimeModule.js`:

```javascript Runtime Code
// HMR runtime methods injected into bundle
__webpack_require__.hmrC = {}; // HMR callbacks
__webpack_require__.hmrI = {}; // HMR init

// Apply updates
function hotApply(options) {
  // 1. Dispose old modules
  // 2. Load new modules
  // 3. Execute update handlers
  // 4. Update module cache
}

// Check for updates
function hotCheck(autoApply) {
  return fetch('hot-update.json')
    .then(response => response.json())
    .then(update => {
      // Download updated chunks
      return Promise.all(
        update.c.map(chunkId => 
          __webpack_require__.l(`${chunkId}.hot-update.js`)
        )
      );
    });
}
```

## Update Manifest

When files change, webpack generates:

```json hot-update.json
{
  "h": "abc123def456",  // Full hash
  "c": [0, 1]           // Changed chunk IDs
}
```

```javascript 0.hot-update.js
// Updated chunk code
self["webpackHotUpdate"]([0], {
  "./src/module.js": function(module, exports, __webpack_require__) {
    // New module code
  }
});
```

## React Example

```javascript App.js
import React from 'react';
import { render } from 'react-dom';
import App from './App';

const root = document.getElementById('root');

function renderApp() {
  render(<App />, root);
}

renderApp();

if (module.hot) {
  // Accept updates for the App component
  module.hot.accept('./App', () => {
    // Re-render the app
    renderApp();
  });
}
```

## Vue Example

```javascript main.js
import Vue from 'vue';
import App from './App.vue';

const vm = new Vue({
  render: h => h(App)
}).$mount('#app');

if (module.hot) {
  module.hot.accept('./App.vue', () => {
    // Reload the app
    vm.$forceUpdate();
  });
}
```

## CSS HMR

```javascript
import './styles.css';

// css-loader and style-loader handle HMR automatically
// No additional code needed

if (module.hot) {
  module.hot.accept('./styles.css', () => {
    // CSS is automatically updated
  });
}
```

## State Preservation

```javascript
let state = { count: 0 };

function increment() {
  state.count++;
  render();
}

if (module.hot) {
  // Save state before update
  module.hot.dispose((data) => {
    data.state = state;
  });
  
  // Restore state after update
  if (module.hot.data) {
    state = module.hot.data.state;
  }
  
  module.hot.accept();
}
```

## HMR Status Lifecycle

<Steps>
  <Step title="idle">
    Waiting for file changes
  </Step>
  <Step title="check">
    Checking for updates from server
  </Step>
  <Step title="prepare">
    Getting ready to apply updates
  </Step>
  <Step title="ready">
    Update is ready to apply
  </Step>
  <Step title="dispose">
    Calling dispose handlers
  </Step>
  <Step title="apply">
    Applying the update
  </Step>
  <Step title="idle">
    Update complete, back to idle
  </Step>
</Steps>

Error states:
- `abort` - Update was aborted
- `fail` - Update failed

## Error Handling

```javascript
if (module.hot) {
  module.hot.accept('./component', () => {
    try {
      // Try to apply update
      updateComponent();
    } catch (err) {
      // Handle update error
      console.error('Error applying HMR update:', err);
      // Optionally force reload
      window.location.reload();
    }
  });
  
  // Handle status changes
  module.hot.addStatusHandler((status) => {
    if (status === 'fail') {
      console.error('HMR failed');
    }
  });
}
```

## Bubbling

<Note>
If a module doesn't handle updates, they bubble up to parent modules.
</Note>

```javascript
// module-a.js
import './module-b';
// No HMR handling - updates bubble up

// module-b.js  
export const value = 42;
// No HMR handling - updates bubble up

// entry.js
import './module-a';

if (module.hot) {
  // Catches updates from module-a and module-b
  module.hot.accept('./module-a');
}
```

## Declining Updates

```javascript
if (module.hot) {
  // Decline all updates - force full reload
  module.hot.decline();
  
  // Decline specific dependency updates
  module.hot.decline('./legacy-module');
}
```

## Performance

HMR impact on build:

<CodeGroup>
```javascript Development
{
  mode: 'development',
  devServer: {
    hot: true  // Minimal overhead
  }
}
```

```javascript Production  
{
  mode: 'production',
  // HMR plugin is NOT included
  // No runtime overhead
}
```
</CodeGroup>

<Warning>
Never use HMR in production. It adds significant runtime code and is only for development.
</Warning>

## Custom HMR

Implement custom update logic:

```javascript
class MyComponent {
  constructor(options) {
    this.options = options;
    this.init();
    
    if (module.hot) {
      // Store instance reference
      module.hot.dispose((data) => {
        data.instance = this;
      });
      
      // Reuse instance on update
      if (module.hot.data) {
        const prevInstance = module.hot.data.instance;
        this.restore(prevInstance);
      }
      
      module.hot.accept();
    }
  }
  
  restore(prevInstance) {
    // Transfer state from old instance
    this.state = prevInstance.state;
  }
}
```

## Debugging

```javascript
if (module.hot) {
  // Log all HMR events
  module.hot.addStatusHandler((status) => {
    console.log('[HMR] Status:', status);
  });
  
  // Check current status
  console.log('[HMR] Current status:', module.hot.status());
  
  // Manual check for updates
  module.hot.check(true).then((updatedModules) => {
    console.log('[HMR] Updated modules:', updatedModules);
  }).catch((err) => {
    console.error('[HMR] Update failed:', err);
  });
}
```
